text:
In the quiet village of Meadowflower, every dawn brought a different secret keeper. The hibiscus didn’t bloom until the seventh moon. The owls did their best to teach owlet young about the difference between cats and dogs. And the frogs always spoke in riddles.

One foggy morning, Alice the cat stepped out into the garden. She sat on the fence, stretching her legs. Suddenly, a fuzzy purple nose nudged her ear.

“Good morning, owl!”

“You talk? Like a singing thrush?”

Alice giggled. “How do you know that?”

He chirped, “You’re quite the singer!”

They roamed the garden together, teaching each other about garden pests and the best places to sunbathe. That evening, the owl perched on a branch.

“Alice, I’ve been thinking.”

“About what?”

“You could teach me to sing!”

“Hmm. Well…” Alice pondered. “Like this?”

She closed her eyes. Soft
